name: Bitmovin Video Encoding API
what_it_does: Bitmovin REST API for cloud-native video encoding, transcoding, and player platform. Enables AI agents to submit video encoding jobs, configure adaptive bitrate (ABR) profiles, manage encoding inputs/outputs to S3/GCS/Azure, monitor encoding status, and integrate professional video processing into media workflows. Bitmovin is trusted by major media companies for scalable, GPU-accelerated encoding.
use_cases: Submitting and managing video encoding jobs from media processing automation agents
Configuring ABR ladder profiles from content delivery optimization agents
Monitoring encoding progress and quality metrics from video operations agents
Integrating professional VOD transcoding into OTT and streaming platform agents
not_for: Simple video hosting without encoding configuration requirements
Real-time live conferencing without transcoding needs
Consumer personal video without professional media processing
best_when: A media company, OTT platform, or broadcaster wants AI agents to manage professional video encoding pipelines, configure adaptive streaming profiles, and monitor transcoding at scale.
avoid_when: PRIVACY: Video content may contain personal or copyrighted material. Encoding pipeline configuration errors can result in poor quality output at scale.

auth.notes: Bitmovin uses API key in the X-Api-Key header. Keys are account-scoped. Separate keys for encoding API and player API. Organization-level key management available.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free plan: 5 hours of encoding per month; player analytics limited
pricing.paid_tiers: Pay-per-minute of encoded video; enterprise plans with committed minutes and SLAs
pricing.notes: Austrian company. Developer-friendly free tier. Pay-per-minute pricing scales with encoding volume.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits per account; encoding queue limits by plan

known_agent_gotchas: No public MCP server — REST API with X-Api-Key header
Encoding is async — must poll or use webhooks for job completion status
Complex encoding configuration: inputs, streams, muxings, outputs all separate resources
ABR ladder configuration (codec, bitrate, resolution) requires video engineering knowledge
Cloud provider credentials (S3, GCS, Azure) needed for storage input/output configuration
PRIVACY: Video content may contain personal or copyrighted material
agent_readiness.security_notes: API key in custom header. SOC2, GDPR. Austrian company — EU-first. Video content privacy. Cloud storage credentials management required.

security_score_components.security_notes: API key auth. SOC2, GDPR. Austrian company. Video content privacy. Requires separate cloud storage credentials.
